sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.exact_document_title: Hepatitis B (chronic): diagnosis and management — Recommendations
sources.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE pathway adaptation
sources.population: Adults, children, young people, and pregnant people with positive or established chronic hepatitis B, with subgroup-specific recommendations.
sources.clinical_setting: Primary and specialist care as qualified by the cited recommendations.
sources.applicability_note: Supports clinician review of hepatitis B results, liver tests, ultrasound, elastography, treatment and monitoring records without interpreting serology or generating referral or treatment.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.3.10 — assessment and liver-disease results
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-reviewed HBsAg, HBeAg, HBV DNA, coinfection, liver blood tests, ultrasound, AFP, elastography, biopsy, pregnancy, and paediatric context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.6.1–1.7.3 — monitoring and surveillance
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording already established monitoring, treatment adverse-effect records, liver status, ultrasound, and surveillance status.
